tasks.title: REA-DRT-07 — Bridge SSR cookie to browser supabase-js session (Realtime never initializes)
tasks.description: DONE — shipped in PR #409 (merged 2026-05-22), live on apex via #412. Remaining: confirm Sentry READY-SET-NEXTJS-1S goes silent with an authenticated apex check. Root cause (investigated 2026-05-19): server-side cookie defaults force httpOnly: true on every cookie they write — including the sb-<ref>-auth-token chunks. src/utils/supabase/server.ts:10-16 and src/utils/supabase/middleware.ts:6-12 both define { httpOnly: true, ...options } defaults, and @supabase/ssr doesn't override httpOnly, so the auth cookies land HttpOnly. The browser supabase-js client (src/utils/supabase/client.ts:19-28) reads cookies via document.cookie, which by spec cannot see HttpOnly cookies — so getSession() returns null, Realtime channels never authenticate, and TokenRefreshService throws REFRESH_FAILED (the symptom #404 patched as graceful failure). Regression introduced in commit f49f6ec5 (2026-01-07 mobile-Safari ITP fix); the sameSite: 'lax' + request+response sync changes in that commit were correct, only httpOnly: true was wrong. Fix: drop httpOnly: true from getDefaultCookieOptions (server.ts) and getCookieOptions (middleware.ts); auth cookies must be JS-readable for the @supabase/ssr SSR↔browser bridge. ~2-line change in 2 files. Add a browser getSession() smoke test in src/lib/auth/__tests__/token-refresh-and-session.test.ts that fails if auth cookies are written HttpOnly. Tradeoff: XSS-exfiltrable auth tokens in principle; mitigated by existing CSP + dompurify + Supabase's short-lived JWT + refresh-token rotation. Keep #404 patch in place — graceful sign-in redirect on legitimate session expiry is still correct behavior. Out of scope: Realtime reconnect handler (see docs/architecture/REMEDIATION_PLAN.md). See docs/JOURNAL.md 2026-05-19 for full trace.

tasks.title: Generalize outbound webhook dispatcher (partner-registry-driven)
tasks.description: Built on feature/catercow-partner-webhooks (commit 50d78873, off development). New src/lib/services/partnerWebhookService.ts reads webhookUrl/webhookSecret from the resolved api_partners row; builds the contract payload, HMAC-SHA256 signs it (x-readyset-signature), 3-attempt backoff (no retry on 4xx), skips with a logged notice when unconfigured. recordAndDispatchLifecycleEvent writes history + dispatches, self-guarding against CARRIER_CONFIGS and non-partner orders. Added getPartnerByOrderNumber (registry, prefix-match, cached). Wired into the driver status route (driverStatus→ASSIGNED/PICKED_UP/ON_THE_WAY/ARRIVED/DELIVERED) and the confirm cancel branch (CANCELLED). 7 unit tests green.

tasks.title: REA-367 — Realtime channel.off() cleanup throws (channel closes ~1ms after subscribe)
tasks.description: RealtimeChannelManager.off() (src/lib/realtime/channels.ts:168-173) calls this.channel.off('broadcast', { event }, listener), but the Supabase RealtimeChannel has no runtime .off() — that's a Phoenix Channels method present only via a .d.ts type augmentation. So cleanup throws TypeError: this.channel.off is not a function; the catch only swallows 'closed' / 'not found' / 'already removed' messages, so it logs a WARN and the channel tears down — the admin driver-locations channel reaches subscribed then closes ~1ms later. Fix: drop the per-event off() path and tear down via client.removeChannel(channel) / channel.unsubscribe() (supabase-js v2 has no per-callback removal). Confirmed live during REA-DRT-07 verification on 2026-05-20 (channel subscribed → cleanup TypeError → closed). Blocks DRT-06+. Promoted from QA fail REA-DRT-05 (sub-step DT5-01, Critical). DONE (PR #410, merged 2026-05-22, live via #412): fixed in PR #410 (stable broadcast dispatcher; removed the phantom channel.off() + its .d.ts augmentation; 38 tests). The TypeError + cleanup WARN are verified gone. The remaining subscribe→close (StrictMode) is split to rea-367b-realtime-strictmode-resubscribe.

tasks.title: Realtime channel closes after subscribe under React StrictMode (no re-subscribe)
tasks.description: Split from REA-367. After the channel.off fix (PR #410), the admin driver-locations channel still reaches subscribed then closes ~1ms later in dev and does not re-subscribe. Root cause is a React StrictMode double-invoke race in useAdminRealtimeTracking (src/hooks/tracking/useAdminRealtimeTracking.ts:512-534): mount 1's deferred .then cleanup (lines 518-523) calls removeChannel('driver-locations') on the channel mount 2 established (the RealtimeClient dedups by channel name), tearing it down with no re-subscribe. StrictMode double-invoke is dev-only, so production single-mount is unaffected — but it blocks dev/QA (DRT-06+). Fix direction: ref-count or guard the shared channel so a stale mount's cleanup can't remove a channel a live mount owns (or key channels per-subscriber). Verify on development.readysetllc.com (prod build, no StrictMode double-invoke) after #410 merges. Found during #410 verification on 2026-05-20.

tasks.title: Durable webhook retry queue (fast-follow)
tasks.description: v1 dispatcher does in-request retries (3 attempts, short backoff). The contract promises “5 attempts over ~30 min”, which needs a queue/cron, not in-request retries. Build a partner_webhook_deliveries table + cron worker on top of the existing order_status_history table. Not a go-live blocker — missed callbacks are recoverable via GET /orders/{id}.
